name: Omen Interpretation
shortDescription: Omen interpretation was a crucial aspect of Babylonian religion and statecraft, involving the deciphering of signs believed to be sent by the gods to foretell the future or provide guidance.
description: Omen interpretation in Babylonian society was a sophisticated and pervasive practice. Babylonians believed that the gods communicated their will through a multitude of observable phenomena, including celestial events (astrology), the behavior of animals, the appearance of newborn creatures (teratoscopy), and the examination of animal entrails (extispicy), particularly the liver. The interpretation of these omens was the responsibility of specialized priests and diviners (baru), who possessed extensive knowledge of omen texts and rituals. These texts meticulously cataloged various signs and their corresponding meanings, offering a framework for understanding divine intentions. The practice was not merely about predicting the future; it was also about identifying potential threats and implementing preventative measures through rituals and offerings to appease the gods. Omen interpretation played a vital role in decision-making at all levels of society, from personal matters to matters of state, influencing political and military strategies. The accuracy of omen interpretation was considered paramount, as misinterpretation could lead to disastrous consequences. The practice reflected a deep-seated belief in the interconnectedness of the divine and the mundane, where every event held potential meaning and significance.

sources.title: Divination and Interpretation of Signs in the Ancient World
sources.author: Amar Annus
